description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by three prominent black metal hoops—one at the top, one at the middle, and one at the bottom. The wood features a rich brown color with visible grain patterns and subtle knots, giving it a rustic and weathered appearance. The metal hoops are slightly worn, showing some texture and patina, suggesting age and durability. The top of the barrel is open, revealing the interior, which appears hollow and consistent with traditional storage barrels. There are no visible handles, spouts, or decorative markings on the exterior. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage yards, or near taverns and markets.
description: A classic wooden barrel with black metal hoops, open at the top and featuring a rustic, weathered wood grain.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, wood grain, and all three black metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: Side view with the wood grain and metal hoops visible, slight shadowing on the right side.
description_view_2: Another side view, similar to view 1 but with different wood grain patterns and lighting.
description_view_3: Rear view, showing the back curvature, wood texture, and metal hoops, with no additional features.
